headline: Berkshire Hathaway Portfolio Under Greg Abel Shifts Toward Alphabet And Delta
summary: Berkshire Hathaway (NYSE:BRK.A) recorded its first quarter of portfolio decisions under new CEO Greg Abel. The company exited positions in Amazon, Visa, Mastercard, and UnitedHealth while sharply reducing the overall number of holdings. Berkshire returned to airline stocks with a new stake in Delta Air Lines and added exposure to Macy's, while reallocating more capital to Alphabet. Berkshire Hathaway enters this new phase with NYSE:BRK.A recently trading around $723,821.0 per share. Over...

headline: Warren Buffett’s Berkshire Hathaway Just More Than Tripled Its Stake in Alphabet
summary: Cash is suddenly king again on Wall Street. With recession fears lingering, interest rates still elevated, and consumers showing signs of fatigue, corporate America has been preparing for rougher weather ahead. Few companies have prepared more aggressively than Berkshire Hathaway (NYSE:BRK-A)(NYSE:BRK-B). The conglomerate ended the first quarter sitting on a staggering $397.6 billion cash pile, ... headline: Berkshire Sold Stocks in First Quarter. 6 Takeaways and a Buffett Mystery.
summary: Warren Buffett’s conglomerate bought $16 billion of stocks and sold $24 billion in the first quarter, a regulatory filing reveals.

headline: Warren Buffett’s Berkshire Big Shakeup Continues: UnitedHealth Stake Completely Sold
summary: For years, investors treated Warren Buffett’s Berkshire Hathaway (NYSE:BRK-A)(NYSE:BRK-B) as the market’s ultimate “follow-the-smart-money” signal. If Buffett bought, retail investors paid attention. If he sold, Wall Street usually started asking uncomfortable questions. Now Berkshire Hathaway has given investors another one to chew on.
